pattern: Simulate the bit-manipulation view: each bit costs one step to shift off (divide by 2), and each set bit costs an extra step to clear (subtract 1), except the leading bit.
hints: Follow the rules directly: while the number is not zero, if it is even divide by two, otherwise subtract one. Count every operation.
Think in binary: dividing by 2 shifts right (one step), subtracting 1 clears the lowest set bit (one step). Each bit contributes steps based on its position and value.
For a k-bit number, the answer equals the number of bits (k) plus the count of 1-bits minus 1, since the leading bit only needs one right-shift to disappear.

edge_inputs.description: one — one subtract to reach zero
power of two — only divisions needed
even number that becomes odd after first halving
